other_observers: I was there with Bill Maynard and Brandon Percival who had it around 10am prior to my arrival.; First found by Dan Maynard on 12/18. Bill Maynard observed the bird on 12/26. Other reports (via CoBirds archives) include David Chartier (1pm, 12/29.2010) and Cici Lee and her husband (11:30am, 12/29/2010).
duration: Multiple short glimpses over a period of 10-15 minutes.
specific_location: A few hundred yards downstream of the foot bridge across the Arkansas River below Pueblo City Park, along the north side of the river. Upstream 100-200 yards from where the small creek drains out of "the olive marsh".
description: See photos. Small, short-tailed wren, warm rusty brown color more or less uniformly colored background with a very faint but slightly paler rusty supercillium, and weakly barred flanks. Overall fairly unmarked. The color of the throat, face, neck and breast, together gave the bird a fairly unpatterned and uniformly colored look. Together, these characters suggest a pretty typical Pacific Wren.   I should add that, at the time, I was unaware of color differences (between the two species) in the barring on the flight feathers and this characteristic was not looked at or documented in the photos.Behaviors: The bird was skulking in root tangles and bushes along the north shore of Arkansas River.Call: The bird made some chatter calls while I was there, those unfortunate vocalizations consistent with both Winter Wren and Pacific Wren.Plumage: Basic; I made no attempt to age the bird
